summary: Problem 9 asks for an explicit, degree-bounded, A-independent polynomial map F characterizing the algebraic relations on scaled determinantal tensors Q^{(αβγδ)} built from generic 3×4 matrices, with a full if-and-only-if proof. With a solvability score of just 16% and no substantive proof attempt on record (the single issue was a seed/placeholder closed as 'no proof to evaluate'), this remains an essentially unstarted research-level problem. The core difficulty is constructing the map and proving both directions of the characterization while controlling degree and ensuring independence from the underlying matrices A^{(i)}.
problems: No actual proof attempt exists — the only tracked issue was a placeholder/seed closed with 'no proof to evaluate', so there is no concrete artifact to evaluate or build upon.
Central mathematical blocker: no explicit construction of the degree-bounded, A-independent polynomial map F on the scaled determinantal tensors has been proposed.
The if-and-only-if characterization is unaddressed — neither the necessity (relations imply vanishing) nor sufficiency (vanishing implies the determinantal structure) direction has been attempted.
Status shows '100 open issues' which is inconsistent with the single resolved seed issue described, suggesting tracker metadata is unreliable or unsynced.
Low solvability score (16%) reflects genuine research-level difficulty in tensor/algebraic-geometry methods (eliminant theory, generic rank conditions over Zariski-open sets).
highlights: The problem is well-specified with clear hypotheses (n≥5, Zariski-generic A^{(i)} ∈ ℝ^{3×4}, target tensors in ℝ^{3×3×3×3}) and an authoritative source (Joe Kileel, UT Austin).
The seed issue was correctly triaged and closed rather than left as a false signal of progress, keeping the tracker honest about the absence of work.
Genericity assumptions provide a clean algebraic-geometry setting (Zariski-open conditions) that makes the relations question well-posed and amenable to elimination-theoretic tooling.

suggested_todos.title: Draft an explicit candidate construction of the polynomial map F on scaled determinantal tensors and verify A-independence on small symbolic instances
Establish the 'easy' (necessity) direction: prove the proposed relations vanish identically on tensors arising from generic 3×4 matrices
Attempt the converse (sufficiency) via elimination/Groebner-basis computation to bound the degree and characterize the ideal of relations
Open a fresh, concrete proof-tracking issue replacing the closed seed placeholder, with explicit subgoals for each proof direction
Reconcile tracker metadata (the '100 open issues' vs single resolved issue discrepancy) to ensure status reporting is accurate
